original_clause.clause_text: The Consultant shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless the Owner from and against all claims, damages, losses, and expenses arising out of or resulting from the performance of the Work, regardless of whether such claim is caused in part by the Owner's negligence.

actions_taken.reasoning: Explicit use of 'indemnify, defend, and hold harmless' associated with performance liability.

actions_taken.description: Unfavorable broad-form indemnification. The clause requires the Consultant to indemnify the Owner 'regardless of whether such claim is caused in part by the Owner's negligence.' Under A/E professional liability guidelines, defending a client for their own negligence is uninsurable.
actions_taken.suggestion: The Consultant shall indemnify and hold harmless the Owner from and against claims, damages, losses, and expenses arising out of the performance of the Work, but only to the extent caused by the negligent acts, errors, or omissions of the Consultant.

original_clause.clause_text: The Consultant's total liability for all claims arising from this Agreement shall not exceed the total fees paid to the Consultant under this Agreement.

actions_taken.reasoning: Clause caps the Consultant's liability to the total fees paid under this Agreement.

actions_taken.description: Favorable. Cap is tied to the total fees paid, which is a standard, highly-insurable baseline for engineering consultants.
actions_taken.suggestion: Original language is favorable and acceptable.

original_clause.clause_text: The Consultant shall maintain Professional Liability Insurance with minimum coverage of $5,000,000 per claim and $10,000,000 aggregate for the duration of this Agreement and for five years following completion.

actions_taken.reasoning: Identifies mandatory Professional Liability coverage limits ($5M per claim / $10M aggregate).

actions_taken.description: High coverage limits ($5,000,000/$10,000,000) combined with a five-year post-completion maintenance requirement. This may exceed standard commercial policies and increase premiums significantly.
actions_taken.suggestion: The Consultant shall maintain Professional Liability Insurance with coverage of $2,000,000 per claim and $2,000,000 aggregate, to be maintained for three years following completion.

original_clause.clause_text: Payment shall be made within sixty (60) days of receipt of a properly submitted invoice. The Owner reserves the right to withhold payment pending resolution of any disputes regarding the quality or completeness of the Work.

actions_taken.reasoning: Specifies net-60 payment terms and outlines owner's dispute withholding rights.

actions_taken.description: Unfavorable payment terms. Sixty (60) days is double the industry standard of thirty (30) days. Furthermore, the unilateral right to withhold payment for vague 'quality disputes' without written notice is a cash flow risk.
actions_taken.suggestion: Payment shall be made within thirty (30) days of receipt of invoice. The Owner must notify the Consultant in writing of any invoice dispute within ten (10) days, and may only withhold payment for the disputed portion.

original_clause.clause_text: The Owner may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon fourteen (14) days written notice. Upon termination, the Consultant shall be compensated for services satisfactorily performed through the termination date, with no additional compensation for lost profits or anticipated fees.

actions_taken.reasoning: Addresses the Owner's rights to terminate for convenience.

actions_taken.description: Owner can terminate for convenience upon a short 14-day notice with no compensation for demobilization costs or wind-down fees, stating 'no additional compensation for lost profits'. This leaves the firm vulnerable to immediate project cancellation costs.
actions_taken.suggestion: The Owner may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon thirty (30) days written notice. In such event, the Consultant shall be paid for all services performed to date plus reasonable termination expenses and demobilization costs.

original_clause.clause_text: The Consultant shall provide complete architectural and engineering design services including but not limited to schematic design, design development, construction documents, and construction administration for the 120,000 GSF laboratory and classroom facility.

actions_taken.reasoning: Describes professional architectural and engineering design deliverables.

actions_taken.description: Favorable. Scope is clearly defined by phase and references standard design phases.

original_clause.clause_text: The Consultant shall indemnify and hold harmless the Owner from all claims arising from the Consultant's negligent acts, errors, or omissions in the performance of professional services. This indemnification shall not extend to claims arising from the Owner's sole negligence.

actions_taken.reasoning: Indemnification clause matching professional services and limiting responsibility to negligence.

actions_taken.description: Favorable. Contains negligence standard ('negligent acts, errors, or omissions') and explicitly excludes owner's sole negligence.

original_clause.clause_text: The Consultant shall maintain Commercial General Liability Insurance of $2,000,000 per occurrence, Professional Liability Insurance of $10,000,000 per claim, and Cyber Liability Insurance of $5,000,000 per claim for the duration of this Agreement.

actions_taken.reasoning: Lists General, Professional, and Cyber Liability insurance levels.

actions_taken.description: Acceptable. While $10M professional liability is high, it is standard for major data center projects and is structured normally.

original_clause.clause_text: Neither party shall be liable for any consequential, incidental, special, or punitive damages arising from or related to this Agreement, regardless of whether such damages are based on warranty, contract, tort, or any other legal theory.

actions_taken.reasoning: Waiver of consequential, incidental, special, or punitive damages.

actions_taken.description: Favorable. The clause represents a mutual waiver of consequential damages, protecting the design firm from massive indirect losses (e.g. data center downtime losses).

original_clause.clause_text: The Consultant shall defend, indemnify, and hold harmless the Owner and the Federal Government from and against any and all claims, damages, and expenses, including attorney's fees, arising from the Consultant's performance of services, to the fullest extent permitted by law.

actions_taken.reasoning: Broad-form duty to defend the Owner and the Federal Government.

actions_taken.description: Unfavorable. Obligates the Consultant to 'defend, indemnify, and hold harmless' both the Owner and the Federal Government, including attorney's fees, before negligence has been established in court. Legal defense costs are uninsurable for professional service firms under standard liability policies.
actions_taken.suggestion: The Consultant shall indemnify and hold harmless the Owner from third-party claims, but only to the extent caused by the negligence of the Consultant. The Consultant shall have no duty to defend the Owner prior to a final adjudication of negligence.

original_clause.clause_text: Payment shall be made within thirty (30) days of receipt of a properly submitted invoice conforming to Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) requirements. Interest on late payments shall accrue at the rate established by the Prompt Payment Act.

actions_taken.reasoning: Specifies Prompt Payment Act interest accrual and net-30 terms.

actions_taken.description: Favorable. Thirty-day terms backed by the federal Prompt Payment Act interest protection represent standard favorable government contract terms.

original_clause.clause_text: All personnel assigned to this project shall possess or obtain a minimum Secret-level security clearance prior to accessing project facilities or Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI). The Consultant shall comply with all applicable NIST SP 800-171 requirements for handling CUI.

actions_taken.reasoning: Requires personnel security clearance and NIST SP 800-171 compliance, falling outside the main operational risk categories.

actions_taken.description: Acceptable. Compliance with federal cybersecurity and access standards is expected for secure facility designs.

original_clause.clause_text: The Consultant's indemnification obligations under this Agreement shall be limited to claims arising from the Consultant's negligent acts, errors, or omissions. The Consultant shall have no obligation to indemnify the Owner for claims arising from the Owner's negligence or the negligence of other parties.

reviews.notes: Original clause contained broad indemnification â€” revised to negligence standard

actions_taken.reasoning: Addresses amended indemnification liabilities.

actions_taken.description: Favorable. Standard negligence baseline ('negligent acts, errors, or omissions') with explicit owner-negligence exclusion. Represents a successful negotiation from CLZ-2025-0001.

original_clause.clause_text: Payment shall be made within forty-five (45) days of receipt of invoice. The Owner may withhold up to 10% of each invoice as retainage, to be released upon substantial completion and final acceptance of all deliverables.

reviews.notes: Retainage clause is non-standard â€” needs legal review

actions_taken.reasoning: Identifies 10% cash withhold retainage.

actions_taken.description: Unfavorable retainage clause. A 10% retainage on professional design services is highly punitive and non-standard, severely restricting cash flow for front-loaded engineering phases.
actions_taken.suggestion: Payment shall be made within thirty (30) days of invoice receipt. No retainage shall be withheld from professional design services invoices.

original_clause.clause_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement for cause upon thirty (30) days written notice if the other party fails to cure a material breach within the notice period. The Owner may terminate for convenience upon twenty-one (21) days notice with compensation for completed work plus reasonable demobilization costs.

actions_taken.reasoning: Specifies cause cure periods and convenience demobilization payments.

actions_taken.description: Favorable. Includes cure periods (30 days) and covers demobilization costs for convenience termination.

original_clause.clause_text: The Consultant shall maintain Professional Liability Insurance with minimum coverage of $2,000,000 per claim and $4,000,000 aggregate. Workers' Compensation Insurance as required by state law. Commercial General Liability of $1,000,000 per occurrence.

actions_taken.reasoning: Lists General, Professional, and Workers Compensation requirements.

actions_taken.description: Favorable. Standard, manageable insurance levels ($2M/$4M professional limit is standard for public education work).

original_clause.clause_text: The Consultant shall provide full MEP engineering design services for the 250,000 SF Tier IV data center facility, including redundant power distribution (2N), precision cooling systems, fire suppression, and building management system integration. Design shall comply with Uptime Institute Tier IV requirements.

reviews.notes: Added BMS integration and Tier IV compliance requirement per client request

actions_taken.reasoning: MEP engineering design deliverables for data center project.

actions_taken.description: Acceptable. Standard technical engineering requirements.

original_clause.clause_text: The Consultant's total aggregate liability under this Agreement shall not exceed two times (2x) the total fees paid under this Agreement. This limitation shall not apply to claims arising from the Consultant's willful misconduct or fraudulent acts.

reviews.notes: 2x multiplier exceeds firm standard â€” recommend capping at 1x total fees

actions_taken.reasoning: Contains 2x fees liability cap.

actions_taken.description: Unfavorable. A 2x fee multiplier exceeds our standard corporate ceiling (1x fees or $100k maximum limit). While limited, the higher ceiling raises organizational risk.
actions_taken.suggestion: The Consultant's total aggregate liability under this Agreement shall not exceed one times (1x) the total fees paid under this Agreement.

original_clause.clause_text: Each party shall indemnify the other party from claims arising from its own negligent acts or omissions. Neither party shall be required to indemnify the other for the other party's negligence. The duty to defend shall run concurrently with the duty to indemnify.

actions_taken.reasoning: Specifies mutual indemnification structures.

actions_taken.description: Favorable. Mutual indemnification tied to negligence standard is standard, balanced language.

original_clause.clause_text: The Owner shall make payment within thirty (30) days of receipt of a properly submitted invoice. No retainage shall be withheld. Late payments shall accrue interest at a rate of 1.5% per month.

actions_taken.reasoning: Specifies payment interest and zero retainage terms.

actions_taken.description: Favorable. Standard net-30 terms with zero retainage and strong interest penalty (1.5% per month) on client late payments.
